title: abutilon mangarevicum
intro: Abutilon mangarevicum, also known as the coral-honeysuckle or tree mallows, is a plant species that belongs to the mallow family (Malvaceae) and is native to the Pacific Islands. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The health benefits associated with Abutilon mangarevicum include its potential to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its ability to act as a natural diuretic and antimicrobial agent. Herbal preparations of Abutilon mangarevicum are typically made by infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea that can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
